key_risks: Semiconductor volatility (SMH 64% annualized vol) could reverse sharply on any AI-spending or earnings disappointment before the July 24 exit.
Hot inflation prints (CPI 4.2% YoY, PCE 4.1%) ahead of July 14 CPI and July 28-29 FOMC could pressure rate-sensitive equities and trigger broad risk-off.
Crowded cyclical/value rotation may reverse if June payrolls (July 2) or durable-goods data disappoint, hurting industrials and financials simultaneously.
Healthcare and financials gains partly reflect recent momentum that could fade; reversal risk if defensive flows unwind.

portfolio.rationale: Strong relative strength with +5.89% 30d return, manufacturing PMI at 49-month high (55.7), and durables ex-transport up 1.3%; cyclical leadership with manageable volatility.
Defensive leadership with +5.25% 30d, low beta (0.40), and rotation into the sector; biotech strength (XBI +13.79%) supports group momentum heading into resilient earnings.
Value outperforming growth with +2.88% 30d vs broad market decline, lower beta (0.76), and tilt toward financials/cyclicals that are strengthening as Nasdaq weakens.
Regional banks (KRE +6.97%) and XLF +3.44% showing strength; steeper curve and solid claims data support credit; July earnings season catalyst for banks.
Micron's blowout FY Q3 (revenue $41.46B vs $23.86B prior) and guidance for ~86% margins and $50B Q4 confirm AI/HBM demand; concrete fundamental catalyst supports the group despite high volatility.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward cyclical and defensive sectors showing genuine relative strength backed by macro catalysts (record manufacturing PMI, healthcare rotation, bank earnings) while taking a measured semiconductor position justified by Micron's exceptional fundamentals rather than price alone.
rationale_summary: Overweight industrials, value, healthcare, and financials for catalyst-backed relative strength, with a fundamentals-driven semiconductor stake from Micron's earnings beat.
